title: Model Cards for Model Reporting
summary: Introduces model cards as structured documentation for intended use, performance characteristics, limitations, and ethical considerations.
auditUse: Use when designing model documentation evidence and checking whether performance claims are broken down by relevant conditions.
citation: Mitchell et al. 2019. Model Cards for Model Reporting. FAT* '19.

title: Datasheets for Datasets
summary: Proposes a standardized process for documenting datasets, including motivation, composition, collection, uses, and maintenance.
auditUse: Use to structure dataset evidence requests and test whether data provenance and limitations are documented.
citation: Gebru et al. 2021. Datasheets for Datasets. Communications of the ACM.

title: Closing the AI Accountability Gap: Defining an End-to-End Framework for Internal Algorithmic Auditing
summary: Defines an internal algorithmic auditing framework across scoping, mapping, artifact collection, testing, and reflection.
auditUse: Use as a backbone for audit program design across the AI development lifecycle.
citation: Raji et al. 2020. Closing the AI Accountability Gap. FAccT '20.

title: AI Auditing: The Broken Bus on the Road to AI Accountability
summary: Taxonomizes current AI audit practices and examines why many audits fail to produce meaningful accountability outcomes.
auditUse: Use to evaluate whether an audit design has institutional context, stakeholder power, and accountability consequences.
citation: Birhane et al. 2024. AI Auditing: The Broken Bus on the Road to AI Accountability. arXiv.

title: A Framework for Assurance Audits of Algorithmic Systems
summary: Proposes a criterion audit framework modeled partly on financial audit assurance practices.
auditUse: Use to think about audit criteria, evidence quality, auditor responsibility, audit opinions, and certification.
citation: Lam et al. 2024. A Framework for Assurance Audits of Algorithmic Systems. FAccT '24.

title: Outsider Oversight: Designing a Third Party Audit Ecosystem for AI Governance
summary: Synthesizes lessons from external oversight systems in other fields to design better AI third-party audit ecosystems.
auditUse: Use when evaluating independence, audit market structure, disclosure, incentives, and third-party access.
citation: Raji et al. 2022. Outsider Oversight. AIES.

title: Sociotechnical Audits: Broadening the Algorithm Auditing Lens to Investigate Targeted Advertising
summary: Extends algorithm auditing by examining interactions between systems and users, not only model inputs and outputs.
auditUse: Use when auditing deployed systems where human behavior, platform context, and downstream effects matter.

title: Governing with Algorithmic Impact Assessments: Six Observations
summary: Examines how algorithmic impact assessments can operate as governance mechanisms and where practice gaps remain.
auditUse: Use to compare audit work with impact assessment and to shape pre-deployment review processes.

title: Assessing the Assessment: Comparing Algorithmic Impact Assessments and AI Audits
summary: Compares audits and impact assessments as complementary governance practices with different accountability functions.
auditUse: Use to decide when the site should route a resource toward audit, impact assessment, or both.
citation: Sloane and Moss. 2023. Assessing the Assessment. SSRN.

title: Fairness and Abstraction in Sociotechnical Systems
summary: Explains abstraction traps that arise when technical fairness work ignores social and institutional context.
auditUse: Use as a guardrail against fairness audits that over-rely on metrics without context.

title: Equality of Opportunity in Supervised Learning
summary: Introduces equal opportunity and equalized odds criteria for supervised learning fairness.
auditUse: Use when reviewing group fairness metrics and their limits in model evaluation.

title: Fairness Beyond Disparate Treatment and Disparate Impact: Learning Classification without Disparate Mistreatment
summary: Introduces disparate mistreatment as a fairness concept based on misclassification rates across groups.
auditUse: Use when checking whether an audit considers error-rate disparities, not only selection-rate disparities.
citation: Zafar et al. 2017. Fairness Beyond Disparate Treatment and Disparate Impact. WWW '17.

title: Hidden Technical Debt in Machine Learning Systems
summary: Frames ML systems as sources of hidden technical debt from data dependencies, entanglement, feedback loops, and configuration issues.
auditUse: Use to identify operational control risks in production ML systems.

summary: Presents HELM as a broad evaluation framework for language models across scenarios and metrics.
auditUse: Use when designing LLM evaluation coverage beyond a single benchmark score.

summary: Introduces the MMLU benchmark for measuring broad multitask language understanding.
auditUse: Use as an example of benchmark design and as a reminder to inspect benchmark relevance to the audited use case.

summary: Reports methods and lessons from red teaming language models to discover harmful behavior.

summary: Describes training AI assistants using constitutional principles and AI feedback rather than direct human labels for every harmful output.

summary: Explains how robotic process automation can be applied to auditing tasks and audit process transformation.
auditUse: Use to connect AI audit workflows with audit automation, repeatable control procedures, and process-level assurance.
citation: Moffitt, Rozario, and Vasarhelyi 2018. Robotic Process Automation for Auditing. Journal of Emerging Technologies in Accounting.

title: Design and Evaluation of an Advanced Continuous Data Level Auditing System: A Three-Layer Structure
source: International Journal of Accounting Information Systems
summary: Designs and evaluates a three-layer continuous data-level auditing system.
auditUse: Use as a foundation for continuous AI audit monitoring, control analytics, and evidence pipeline design.
citation: Yoon et al. 2021. Design and Evaluation of an Advanced Continuous Data Level Auditing System. International Journal of Accounting Information Systems.

title: Actionable Auditing: Investigating the Impact of Publicly Naming Biased Performance Results of Commercial AI Products
summary: Studies the impact of publicly naming biased commercial AI products after external audit results.
auditUse: Use to understand how public audit disclosure can create accountability pressure and drive vendor behavior change.
citation: Raji and Buolamwini 2019. Actionable Auditing. AIES '19.

title: Ethics-Based Auditing of Automated Decision-Making Systems: Intervention Points and Policy Implications
summary: Analyzes where ethics-based audit interventions can operate across automated decision-making systems and organizational governance.
auditUse: Use to identify intervention points for ethics reviews, documentation controls, and governance integration.
citation: Mokander and Axente 2021. Ethics-Based Auditing of Automated Decision-Making Systems. arXiv.

title: Towards AI Accountability Infrastructure: Gaps and Opportunities in AI Audit Tooling
summary: Maps the AI audit tooling ecosystem and identifies gaps between available tools and accountability-oriented audit practice.
auditUse: Use when evaluating whether tools support actual audit objectives beyond narrow technical testing.
citation: Ojewale et al. 2024. Towards AI Accountability Infrastructure. arXiv.

title: Auditing of AI: Legal, Ethical and Technical Approaches
summary: Reviews legal, ethical, and technical approaches to AI auditing and argues for structured, holistic audit procedures.
auditUse: Use as an orientation paper for comparing process audits, model audits, ethics audits, and legal compliance audits.
citation: Mokander 2024. Auditing of AI. arXiv.

title: An Institutional View of Algorithmic Impact Assessments
summary: Analyzes algorithmic impact assessments as institutional governance tools rather than only technical checklists.
auditUse: Use to think about how AIA procedures fit into agencies, organizations, disclosure systems, and accountability institutions.
citation: Selbst 2021. An Institutional View of Algorithmic Impact Assessments. Harvard Journal of Law & Technology.

title: Data Statements for Natural Language Processing
source: Transactions of the Association for Computational Linguistics
summary: Proposes data statements to document language datasets, including curation rationale, speaker demographics, and recommended uses.
auditUse: Use to strengthen language-data documentation and assess whether NLP data limitations are visible to model users.
citation: Bender and Friedman 2018. Data Statements for Natural Language Processing. TACL.

title: Data Cards: Purposeful and Transparent Dataset Documentation for Responsible AI
summary: Introduces Data Cards as human-centered documentation artifacts for ML datasets across practical organizational contexts.
auditUse: Use when designing concise dataset documentation for different audit audiences and lifecycle stages.
citation: Pushkarna, Zaldivar, and Kjartansson 2022. Data Cards. FAccT '22.

title: Auditing Large Language Models: A Three-Layered Approach
summary: Proposes governance, model, and application audit layers for large language models.
auditUse: Use to scope LLM audits across provider governance, base-model evaluation, and deployed application controls.
citation: Mokander et al. 2023. Auditing Large Language Models. arXiv.

title: Black-Box Access is Insufficient for Rigorous AI Audits
summary: Argues that black-box model access alone is inadequate for rigorous audits and compares black-box, white-box, and outside-the-box access.
auditUse: Use when specifying auditor access rights, evidence needs, confidentiality protections, and audit scope limitations.
citation: Casper et al. 2024. Black-Box Access is Insufficient for Rigorous AI Audits. arXiv.

title: HarmBench: A Standardized Evaluation Framework for Automated Red Teaming and Robust Refusal
summary: Introduces HarmBench as a standardized benchmark for automated red teaming and robust refusal evaluation.
auditUse: Use to benchmark harmful-request refusal and red-team robustness across LLMs and defenses.
citation: Mazeika et al. 2024. HarmBench. arXiv.

title: Preventing Repeated Real World AI Failures by Cataloging Incidents: The AI Incident Database
summary: Introduces the AI Incident Database as a shared memory for real-world failures of intelligent systems.
auditUse: Use to seed incident taxonomies, post-deployment monitoring plans, and lessons-learned repositories.
citation: McGregor 2020. Preventing Repeated Real World AI Failures by Cataloging Incidents. arXiv.

title: Emerging Practices in Frontier AI Safety Frameworks
summary: Summarizes emerging practices for writing frontier AI safety frameworks after the AI Seoul Summit commitments.
auditUse: Use to compare frontier developer safety frameworks across risk identification, mitigation, and governance practices.
citation: Buhl, Bucknall, and Masterson 2025. Emerging Practices in Frontier AI Safety Frameworks. arXiv.

title: A Frontier AI Risk Management Framework: Bridging the Gap Between Current AI Practices and Established Risk Management
summary: Proposes a risk management framework for frontier AI inspired by established high-risk industry practices.
auditUse: Use when evaluating frontier AI developer risk identification, risk analysis, mitigation, and governance processes.
citation: Campos et al. 2025. A Frontier AI Risk Management Framework. arXiv.
